title: yosys-smtbmc: Contradictory "Cover" Results
body:
This is a repost of issue [#706](https://github.com/YosysHQ/yosys/issues/706)

I have a simple [design ](https://github.com/hotwolf/WbXbc/blob/master/rtl/verilog/WbXbc_address_decoder.v) with a[ test bench](https://github.com/hotwolf/WbXbc/blob/master/bench/verilog/ftb_WbXbc_address_decoder.sv), that contains two cover statements:

```
cover (wb_itr_mon_fsm_busy & $past(wb_itr_mon_fsm_busy));
cover (wb_itr_mon_fsm_busy & ~rst & req & ack);
```
The first one (trying to prove, that the bus monitor can assert the signal "wb_itr_mon_fsm_busy" for two consecutive cycles) is reported as unreachable.
The second cover statement produces a witness trace, showing signal "wb_itr_mon_fsm_busy" asserted for two consecutive cycles (pls. see [screenshot](https://hotwolf.github.io/WbXbc/images/issue706.png)). This contradicts the result of the first cover statement.

To run my test case, please execute the following steps:
```
git clone https://github.com/hotwolf/WbXbc.git
cd WbXbc
make cover.WbXbc_address_decoder
make debug
```

(Note: "make debug" requires the installation of Perl and the Verilog-Perl library. Alternatively, run "make debug.list" to see the location of the VCD file and run gtkwave manually)
